---
title: "Off-grid power, solar, and energy harvesting"
chapter-id: S09
status: outline
---

::: {.callout-note title="Chapter outline"}
The scope and dependency boundary are stable. Full exposition, worked examples,
exercises, and tested practical evidence still need authoring and review.
:::

## Central question

How do you keep a system running from an unreliable or absent grid within a
truthful energy budget?

## Learning outcomes

After completing this chapter, the reader should be able to:

- construct an energy budget for a load profile served by a variable source;
- size a photovoltaic source, storage, and conversion stage for a stated
  availability target;
- select and configure charge control and protection for the chosen storage
  chemistry;
- evaluate whether ambient harvesting can sustain a duty-cycled load, and bound
  the resulting claim.

## Concept path

- source characterization: PV curves, irradiance, temperature, and derating;
- maximum-power-point tracking and conversion stages;
- storage sizing, depth of discharge, and lifetime;
- charge control, protection, and safe disconnection;
- harvesting from light, heat, vibration, and radio energy;
- availability, autonomy, and honest energy accounting.

## Evidence and practice

The completed chapter will include a prediction, a worked model, a simulation or
executable check where it adds value, measured or authentic data, a fault or
model mismatch, and a requirements-based design decision. The final exercise set
will mix retrieval, calculation, interpretation, debugging, and open design.

## Connections

Chapter ID: `S09`. Prerequisites and downstream course mappings are maintained in
[`chapters.toml`](../chapters.toml). The [reading roadmap](../roadmap.qmd)
provides reader-facing routes and the practice spine.
